title: Day 22 — Khamenei Declares 'Dizzying Blow' as Trump Rejects Ceasefire; Iran Launches 67th Wave; Switzerland Halts US Arms Exports; US Siege of Iranian Economy Tightens
detail: Day 22 of Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a public statement claiming Iran had delivered a 'dizzying blow' to the enemy, characterizing the conflict as a strategic victory for the Islamic Republic despite the massive military losses. Trump categorically rejected any ceasefire in response, saying: 'There is no ceasefire as long as Iran refuses to surrender.' Iran launched its 67th retaliatory wave (Operation True Promise 4 continued), deploying a fresh barrage of ballistic missiles and drones targeting Israeli population centers and US bases in the Gulf. The IRGC stated the operation targeted military positions 'in the heart of occupied territories and US forward bases.' Switzerland — which has served as a US diplomatic intermediary to Iran — announced it was halting US arms export authorizations and closing its airspace to US military flights linked to Iran operations, citing neutrality obligations and humanitarian law concerns. This extraordinary Swiss move signals growing international isolation of the US-Israel campaign. Trump, in turn, publicly criticized NATO allies as 'cowards' for insufficient support in securing the Strait of Hormuz. The US-Israel campaign now stands at over 7,200 targets struck across Iran in 22 days. Iran's missile launch rate has declined to approximately 20–25 per day, down 93% from the Day 1 peak of ~350, reflecting the severe degradation of its launch infrastructure. Reports from inside Iran describe growing internal pressure despite the blackout: opposition groups and civil society actors are calling for an end to resistance. Brent crude traded near $112/bbl as Qatar's LNG production remained offline and the global energy crisis deepened.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei: Iran Delivered 'Dizzying Blow' to Enemy; Trump Says No Ceasefire
detail: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — appointed after his father Ali Khamenei was killed on Day 1 (Feb 28) — issued a written statement declaring Iran had delivered a 'dizzying blow' to the enemy and that the nation had demonstrated its willingness to resist. The statement, broadcast on Iranian state media despite near-total internet blackout, was his most comprehensive public address since assuming the role on March 8. Khamenei framed the conflict in terms of long-term strategic resistance, saying: 'Every martyr's blood fertilizes the roots of resistance. The enemy believed it could break Iran in days. Instead, it has revealed its true face to the world.' Trump responded directly, telling reporters: 'There is no ceasefire as long as Iran refuses to surrender. Anytime I want the war to end it will end — but it will end on our terms.' The exchange underscores the complete absence of a negotiated off-ramp and signals the conflict will continue through at minimum the end of March 2026. Turkey's FM Hakan Fidan separately confirmed Ankara continues to maintain open diplomatic channels to both sides but acknowledged 'the current conditions for a ceasefire do not exist.'

title: Iran Launches 67th Wave — IRGC Targets Israeli Cities and US Gulf Bases
detail: The IRGC launched the 67th wave of Operation True Promise 4, targeting Israeli population centers in central Israel and US military forward bases across the Gulf. The barrage included approximately 18 ballistic missiles and 24 drones. Gulf air defense systems — including THAAD batteries in Saudi Arabia and the UAE's Patriot network — intercepted a significant portion. The IRGC Aerospace Force confirmed the strike and stated it targeted 'military and intelligence infrastructure in the heart of occupied territories.' Israeli air raid sirens were activated across central and southern Israel. The IDF reported 4 missiles reached populated areas in central Israel, causing structural damage to residential buildings and wounding 6 civilians near Rishon LeZion. No Israeli fatalities were reported from this specific wave. The trajectory and volume of the 67th wave — at roughly 42 total projectiles — represents a further reduction from peak launch rates but a continued qualitative focus on precision and penetration. Analysts note the IRGC is increasingly reserving its most capable ballistic missile systems for high-value psychological impact strikes.

title: Switzerland Halts US Arms Export Authorizations, Closes Airspace to US Military Flights Linked to Iran Campaign
detail: Switzerland — which has historically served as a diplomatic intermediary between the US and Iran, hosting the US interests section — announced it is halting all pending US arms export authorizations under Swiss law and closing Swiss airspace to US military flights directly linked to the Iran campaign, citing its status as a permanently neutral state and obligations under international humanitarian law. Swiss Federal Councillor Karin Keller-Sutter stated: 'Switzerland's neutrality is not passive. It requires us to take no actions that provide material military advantage to any belligerent power.' The Swiss Federal Council noted it had received a US request to allow military transport overflights, which was denied. Switzerland's break with the US is the most dramatic act by a European neutral since the war began and creates a precedent that could influence Austria, Ireland, and other European neutrals. Switzerland had previously signaled concern about the legality of the Minab school strike (Day 8) and the broader campaign's targeting of civilian infrastructure. The US State Department described the Swiss decision as 'deeply disappointing and contrary to the spirit of our longstanding relationship.' Switzerland still maintains its diplomatic mission in Tehran on behalf of US interests.

title: Trump Calls NATO Allies 'Cowards' for Failing to Deploy Warships to Hormuz; Demands Partners Share Burden
detail: President Trump publicly criticized NATO allies as 'cowards' for insufficient action in securing the Strait of Hormuz, escalating pressure on Europe and Asia to contribute naval forces. In remarks at the White House, Trump stated: 'These NATO countries, they sit back and watch us do all the work. They are cowards. They need to send their warships.' The outburst came days after the UK, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, and Japan issued a joint statement expressing willingness to join 'appropriate efforts' for safe Hormuz passage (Day 21) — but have not yet deployed warships. NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said the Alliance was 'examining options' but noted Iran has not attacked NATO-member shipping directly. Trump's frustration reflects the reality that the Hormuz selective blockade — which allows Chinese and Indian vessels but bars US, Israeli, and many Western-allied ships — is beginning to damage Gulf state economies that are ostensibly US partners. The QatarEnergy CEO's warning against attacking energy infrastructure and Qatar's LNG production halt have added urgency to Trump's pressure campaign. Analysts note that unilateral US-only Hormuz operations are straining CENTCOM resources already committed to the Iran air campaign.

title: US-Israel Strike Day 22: 7,200+ Total Targets — Continued Campaign Against Military and Leadership Targets
detail: US and Israeli forces continued the Day 22 strikes against Iran, bringing the cumulative target count to over 7,200 since February 28. The strikes focused on remaining missile infrastructure, IRGC command nodes, and suspected leadership hideouts. CENTCOM confirmed additional strikes on hardened launch sites in southeastern Iran — a region that had previously been less targeted, suggesting intelligence updates on surviving launcher locations. IDF continued targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in Lebanon, with fresh strikes on Bekaa Valley arms depots and Tyre area positions. Lebanon's death toll continued to climb, with reports of additional civilian casualties near IDF strike zones in southern Lebanon. The IDF announced it had now struck approximately 73% of Iran's identified mobile missile launcher fleet and 87% of fixed radar and air defense installations. Pentagon spokesman stated the campaign was 'precisely on track' toward the stated objectives of neutralizing Iran's nuclear and missile program. Defense Secretary Hegseth reiterated that no Iranian offer has met the threshold for ceasefire negotiations. US intelligence officials privately assessed to Congress that Iran's missile launch capacity would fall below 5–10 per day within 7–10 days at current degradation rates.

title: Signals of Internal Pressure Inside Iran Despite Blackout — Opposition Groups Call for End to Resistance
detail: Despite Iran's near-total internet blackout (now exceeding 310 hours at approximately 1% connectivity), reports filtering out via satellite phones, VPNs, and the diaspora suggest growing internal pressure on the Iranian government. Multiple opposition organizations based abroad — including the Iran Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) and Hengaw — reported that citizens in Tehran, Isfahan, and Tabriz have staged small street protests calling for an end to the war, shouting 'Neither Gaza nor Lebanon — our life for Iran.' HRANA documented at least 14 arrests of anti-war protesters in Tehran's Yusef Abad neighborhood on Day 21 (March 20). The Iranian government has deployed plainclothes Basij forces across major cities to suppress gatherings following Nowruz. The IRGC's Basij organization has itself suffered significant personnel losses in strikes on command facilities. Iranian FM Araghchi's continued flat rejection of ceasefire talks may mask deeper divisions within the system: Iran's parliament (Majlis) reportedly convened a closed-door session on Day 21–22 to assess the situation, according to a single source cited by Iran International (tier 4, unverified).

title: Brent Crude Near $112 as Qatar LNG Remains Offline — Global Energy Crisis Deepens
detail: Brent crude settled near $112/bbl on Day 22 as Qatar's LNG production remained fully halted — the second consecutive day without output from one of the world's top LNG producers. Asian LNG spot prices surged 18% overnight as European and Asian buyers scrambled for alternative supply from Australia, the United States, and Russia. European natural gas (TTF benchmark) futures jumped 22% in their largest single-day move in 14 months. Fertilizer prices — heavily dependent on natural gas as feedstock — rose 15–20% on global commodity exchanges. The UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) issued an emergency alert warning that the Qatar LNG halt could push food prices to record highs by mid-2026 if production is not restored within two weeks. The IEA's coordinated strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) release of 400 million barrels (announced earlier in the conflict) is providing partial cushion for oil markets but does not address the LNG gap. Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan — almost entirely dependent on Persian Gulf energy imports — activated national energy emergency protocols. The US Energy Department confirmed it is accelerating LNG export authorizations from Gulf Coast terminals to partially offset the shortfall.
